extraction.one_line_summary: KRON Andreas won from a late 4-rider move that went unchallenged on the final climb, beating SÁNCHEZ Luis León in the sprint.
tactical_patterns: MOVISTAR used PEDRERO/VERONA/SAMITIER to pace hard on climb 4 explicitly to drop SAGAN, then inexplicably sat up when SÁNCHEZ counterattacked.
BORA used SCHELLING to pace mid-race despite his climbing strength being better saved for positioning rather than domestique work.
BIKE EXCHANGE failed to control when 4-rider move formed, only reacting with CHAVES on final climb when gap reached 35 seconds.
KÄMNA attacked the 4-rider group but JUMBO controlled his gap rather than letting him solo, prioritizing stage win over GC time.

rider_signals.KRON Andreas: positive: Won first World Tour race with superior positioning, drafted ROCHAS' early launch to beat faster finisher SÁNCHEZ.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Dropped on 6% gradient section with 5km left on penultimate climb, signaling poor climbing condition compared to expectations.
rider_signals.SCHELLING Ide: positive: Still finished 7th after extended pacing work, showing depth that suggests misallocation rather than weakness.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Popped on false flat after main climb descent when MOVISTAR pace intensified, not on steep gradient itself.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: MOVISTAR positioned him second wheel blocking chase when SÁNCHEZ attacked, tactics prioritized teammate over his sprint.
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: negative: Left alone by BIKE EXCHANGE to chase 35-second gap on final climb, too late to be effective.
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: Strong enough to attack 4-rider group but JUMBO managed gap, indicating GC form not just stage hunting.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: Lost wheels on descent but recaught, suggesting MOVISTAR descent pace was genuinely threatening even to climbers.
